entries.definition: an act of breaking or failing to observe a law, agreement, promise, or code of conduct
entries.exampleSentence: The company was sued for breach of contract after failing to deliver the goods on time.

entries.definition: a gap in a wall, barrier, or defence, especially one made by an attacking force
entries.exampleSentence: The defenders rushed to repair the breach in the castle wall before another assault.

entries.definition: to break through or break a rule, agreement, or barrier
entries.exampleSentence: The hackers managed to breach the company's security systems.

entries.definition: to rise and break through the surface of the water (of a whale)
entries.exampleSentence: We watched in awe as a humpback whale breached right beside our boat.
